Effective DateSection effective , except with respect to certain noncompetitive programs and competitive programs, see , set out as an Effective Date of 2015 Amendment note under .Dec. 10, 2015section 5 of Pub. L. 114–95section 6301 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: create, develop, implement, replicate, or take to scale entrepreneurial, evidence-based, field-initiated innovations to improve student achievement and attainment for high-need students; and

children.label: B
children.text: rigorously evaluate such innovations, in accordance with subsection (e).

children.label: A
children.text: early-phase grants to fund the development, implementation, and feasibility testing of a program, which prior research suggests has promise, for the purpose of determining whether the program can successfully improve student achievement or attainment for high-need students;

children.label: B
children.text: mid-phase grants to fund implementation and a rigorous evaluation of a program that has been successfully implemented under an early-phase grant described in subparagraph (A) or other effort meeting similar criteria, for the purpose of measuring the program’s impact and cost effectiveness, if possible using existing administrative data; and

children.label: i
children.text: determining whether such impacts can be successfully reproduced and sustained over time; and

children.label: ii
children.text: identifying the conditions in which the program is most effective.
A local educational agency.
A State educational agency.
The Bureau of Indian Education.
A consortium of State educational agencies or local educational agencies.
A nonprofit organization.

children.label: A
children.text: a nonprofit organization;

children.label: B
children.text: a business;

children.label: C
children.text: an educational service agency; or

children.label: D
children.text: an institution of higher education.

children.label: i
children.text: a local educational agency with an urban-centric district locale code of 32, 33, 41, 42, or 43, as determined by the Secretary;

children.label: ii
children.text: a consortium of such local educational agencies;

children.label: iii
children.text: an educational service agency or a nonprofit organization in partnership with such a local educational agency; or

children.label: iv
children.text: a grantee described in clause (i) or (ii) in partnership with a State educational agency.

children.label: B
children.text: A majority of the schools to be served by the program are designated with a locale code of 32, 33, 41, 42, or 43, or a combination of such codes, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: 2 Exception
children.text: Notwithstanding paragraph (1), the Secretary shall reduce the amount of funds made available under such paragraph if the Secretary does not receive a sufficient number of applications of sufficient quality.
the difficulty of raising matching funds for a program to serve a rural area;

children.label: A
children.text: who are in poverty, as counted in the most recent census data approved by the Secretary;

children.label: B
children.text: who are eligible for a free or reduced price lunch under the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act ( et seq.);42 U.S.C. 1751

children.label: C
children.text: whose families receive assistance under the State program funded under part A of title IV of the Social Security Act ( et seq.); or42 U.S.C. 601

children.label: D
children.text: who are eligible to receive medical assistance under the Medicaid program; and
the difficulty of raising funds on tribal land.

children.heading: Evaluation
children.label: e Evaluation
children.text: Each recipient of a grant under this section shall conduct an independent evaluation of the effectiveness of the program carried out under such grant.
provide technical assistance for eligibility entities, which may include pre-application workshops, web-based seminars, and evaluation support; and
to disseminate best practices.
